description: Telemetry schema in pibo.sqlite, typed store/service APIs, session/event correlation links, provider event volume controls, preview-disabled contract, retention stats, and pruning for Pibo debug telemetry.

userStories.title: Add telemetry record types and correlation model
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I want shared telemetry TypeScript types so that capture, store, and CLI code use the same correlated record shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add TypeScript types for telemetry turns, phases, provider requests, provider event metadata/aggregates, tool calls, retention classes including incident, preview-unavailable results, and query results

Types include applicable correlation fields for piboSessionId, rootSessionId, roomId, turnId, phaseId, normalized event id/row id, payload metadata id, providerRequestId, upstreamResponseId, toolCallId, toolName, runId, eventStreamId, createdAt, and updatedAt
Types model status values for running, ok, error, aborted, timeout, open, and stale-equivalent query states where applicable
Types model telemetry default-on/best-effort service behavior without requiring callers to persist content bodies
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage foundation batch on 2026-05-16: added shared TypeScript telemetry types, correlation fields, status enums, retention classes including incident, preview-unavailable result type, and best-effort service wrapper. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test.

userStories.title: Add idempotent telemetry schema migration
userStories.description: As a storage maintainer, I want telemetry tables created additively so that existing Pibo stores keep working.
acceptanceCriteria: Add idempotent pibo.sqlite migrations for telemetry turns, phases, provider requests, provider event summaries/aggregates, tool calls, retention classes, and optional preview metadata only if explicitly enabled
Add indexes for lookup by piboSessionId, roomId, event id/row id, payload metadata id, turnId, providerRequestId, toolCallId, createdAt, updatedAt, and retention class where applicable
Schema supports bidirectional lookup from sessions/events/payload metadata to telemetry and from telemetry back to session/event evidence
Schema includes retention classes for live, diagnostic, incident, and payload_preview where applicable
Running migrations twice succeeds without duplicate-column or duplicate-table failures
Existing debug/session/event store tests continue to pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage foundation batch on 2026-05-16: added additive pibo.sqlite telemetry tables and indexes for turns, phases, provider requests, provider event summaries, tool calls, retention classes, and session/event/payload/provider/tool lookup paths. Migration idempotency covered by tests. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test.

userStories.title: Implement telemetry store write APIs
userStories.description: As runtime capture code, I want small store methods so that telemetry writes do not require raw SQL at capture sites.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement typed write/update APIs for starting/updating turns, phases, provider requests, provider event summaries/aggregates, tool calls, and optional bounded previews
Write APIs are safe to call repeatedly for the same logical id where upsert semantics are expected
Telemetry is enabled by default through the service wrapper, but unavailable store or write failures do not crash callers
Store contract tests cover insert, update, terminal status, and missing optional correlation ids
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage foundation batch on 2026-05-16: implemented typed upsert/write APIs for turns, phases, provider requests, provider event summaries/counters, tool calls, preview-disabled reads, and a best-effort wrapper that swallows unavailable-store/write failures. Store contract tests cover insert/update/terminal status/missing optional correlation ids. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test.

userStories.title: Implement telemetry store read APIs for debug commands
userStories.description: As a CLI engineer, I want bounded telemetry queries so that debug commands can render summaries without scanning or dumping entire stores.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement read APIs for listing telemetry sessions, inspecting one session, reading a turn timeline, reading a provider request, listing provider event metadata/aggregates with cursor/limit, reading a tool call, listing stale work, reading stats, and returning preview-unavailable results by default
List APIs enforce default limits and hard maximum limits
Provider event listing supports sequence or cursor-based paging
Read API tests cover unknown ids, empty stores, limits, cursors, and correlation lookup
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage read/retention batch on 2026-05-16: added bounded read APIs for telemetry sessions, session details, turn timelines, provider event cursor pages, stale work, stats, and preview-unavailable reads. APIs enforce default/hard limits and provider event sequence cursors. Tests cover unknown ids, bounded session/turn reads, cursors, and correlation lookup. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test (406 passing tests).

userStories.title: Add centralized payload volume-control helper for telemetry
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want one bounded preview/truncation helper so telemetry does not duplicate large payloads or full tool/session content.
acceptanceCriteria: Helper enforces byte limits and reports truncation metadata for any optional preview path
Default telemetry persistence stores metadata, counters, ids, timestamps, statuses, and links rather than full content bodies
Tests cover large JSON objects, arrays, headers, text payloads, and tool arguments to prove default storage remains bounded
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage read/retention batch on 2026-05-16: added centralized telemetry bounded-preview and safe-field/top-level-key helpers for optional preview paths and header/payload/tool-argument display values. Tests cover large JSON-like tool args, header-like values, truncation metadata, and safe field/key extraction while default persistence remains metadata-only. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test (406 passing tests).

userStories.title: Implement preview-disabled and optional bounded preview contract
userStories.description: As an operator, I want preview paths to be explicitly disabled/unavailable by default so telemetry cannot accidentally become a raw payload store.
acceptanceCriteria: Default preview capture and preview persistence are disabled/unavailable unless an explicit service option enables bounded previews
Preview read APIs return a typed unavailable/disabled result by default and never fall back to arbitrary raw provider payload reads

If a later explicit preview mode is enabled, saved previews include owner kind, owner id, byte size, truncation flag, volume-control flag, content type, preview text, createdAt, and payload_preview retention class
Preview text, when enabled, is capped by a default byte limit and a hard maximum limit
Tests prove disabled preview capture and reads return clear unavailable results
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage read/retention batch on 2026-05-16: kept V1 preview capture disabled by default, preserved typed disabled/unavailable preview reads, and added bounded preview helper types for any later explicit preview mode without falling back to arbitrary raw provider payload reads. Tests prove disabled reads and bounded preview behavior. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test (406 passing tests).

userStories.title: Add telemetry retention stats and prune service
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want stats and dry-run pruning so that telemetry storage can be managed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement telemetry stats grouped by retention class, including live, diagnostic, incident, and payload_preview where present, with row counts and byte counts where available
Implement prune dry-run that reports rows and bytes that would be deleted for a retention class and cutoff
Implement explicit prune apply that deletes only matching telemetry rows and does not affect sessions, transcripts, or normalized event logs
Tests cover dry-run, apply, cutoff filtering, retention class filtering, and unrelated data preservation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in storage read/retention batch on 2026-05-16: added telemetry retention stats grouped by retention class/table with byte counts where available, dry-run-first prune planning, and explicit apply pruning for telemetry tables only. Tests cover stale read, stats, dry-run, apply, cutoff/retention filtering, and preservation of unrelated retention classes. Validated with Docker npm run typecheck and npm test (406 passing tests).
